summary: Russian forces launched multiple KAB attacks on Ukrainian settlements, striking Vasylkivka, Mykolaiv, Kherson and an oil processing plant in Volgograd region, Russia. Ukrainian UAVs hit the Vostochny training ground in Zaporizhzhia, destroying air defenses and targeting enemy personnel, and repeatedly struck bridges linking Crimea to the mainland. Air defence units reported high interception rates, downing over 100 Ukrainian drones across Russian territory and the Black Sea.
key_points: Russian KABs hit Vasylkivka market, injuring nine and damaging stores and apartments (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Ukrainian UAVs struck the Vostochny training ground in Zaporizhzhia, destroying TOR‑M2, ZU‑23, MANPADS and enemy troops (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
Four Shahed drones launched from the Black Sea were intercepted over Chornomorsk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67592).
Russian air defence reported shooting down 109 Ukrainian drones over Russian regions and the Black Sea in one day (Source: @tass_agency/380533).
Ukrainian forces hit bridges connecting Crimea to the mainland with multiple strikes (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
Russian KABs were launched in northeastern Kharkiv and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/65118).
An oil processing facility in Kotovo, Volgograd region, Russia was hit, causing a fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81894).
Russian pilots destroyed Ukrainian temporary dislocation points in the DPR using FAB‑3000 bombs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875).

sections.executive_summary: Russian KAB attacks on Vasylkivka, Mykolaiv, Kherson and a Volgograd oil plant caused civilian injuries and infrastructure damage. Ukrainian UAVs successfully hit the Vostochny training ground in Zaporizhzhia, destroying air defence systems and targeting enemy forces, and repeatedly struck Crimea‑linking bridges. Air defence exchanges remain intense, with Russia reporting over 100 Ukrainian drone interceptions in a single day.

points: Ukrainian UAVs attacked the Vostochny training ground in Zaporizhzhia, destroying TOR‑M2 and ZU‑23 air defence missiles, a MANPADS battery and enemy personnel (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
Multiple UAV strikes were reported on bridges connecting Crimea to the mainland, disrupting Russian logistical routes (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
A Shahed drone was observed over Mykolaiv, indicating continued low‑altitude strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67593).
UAVs launched from the Black Sea towards Odesa and the Zatoka area were tracked, showing Russian use of maritime launch points (Source: @kpszsu/65090).
Ukrainian forces employed reactive UAVs near Zolochiv in Kharkiv, heading towards Prudyanka (Source: @kpszsu/65069).
UAVs were sighted heading to Zaporizhzhia from the southwest, suggesting pressure on the city’s airspace (Source: @kpszsu/65126).
UAV activity was reported west of Dnipro, prompting engagement by air defence assets (Source: @kpszsu/65105).
UAVs were observed over Dnipropetrovsk heading past Krynychky and towards Pavlohrad from the southeast (Source: @kpszsu/65100).
Russian KAB rockets struck Vasylkivka market, injuring nine civilians and damaging eight shops, ten market pavilions and a multi‑storey building (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
KAB attacks were launched in the northeastern part of Kharkiv and in Donetsk, indicating expanded strike zones (Source: @kpszsu/65118).
Repeated KAB launches were reported east of Dnipropetrovsk and in Donetsk, showing sustained offensive pressure (Source: @kpszsu/65071).
An oil processing facility in Kotovo, Volgograd region, Russia was hit, igniting a fire at the plant that handles oil from Volgograd, Astrakhan and Kalmykia (Source: @UkraineNow/81894).
Russian pilots used FAB‑3000 bombs to destroy Ukrainian temporary dislocation points in the DPR (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30875).
Four Shahed drones launched from the Black Sea were intercepted over Chornomorsk, reflecting Russian air defence response (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67592).
Russian air defence reported downing 109 Ukrainian drones over Russian territory and the Black Sea in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/380533).
A drone strike in Mykolaiv caused injuries to two civilians and a child experiencing stress reactions (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).

key_developments: Russian KAB rockets struck Vasylkivka market, injuring nine civilians and damaging multiple shops and a residential building (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Ukrainian UAVs destroyed air defence systems (TOR‑M2, ZU‑23, MANPADS) at the Vostochny training ground in Zaporizhzhia, also targeting enemy personnel (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
Four Shahed drones launched from the Black Sea were intercepted over Chornomorsk, highlighting maritime UAV launch routes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67592).
Russian air defence reported downing 109 Ukrainian drones over Russian territory and the Black Sea in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/380533).
Ukrainian strikes hit bridges linking Crimea to the mainland, disrupting Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
An oil processing facility in Kotovo, Volgograd region, Russia was hit, causing a fire at a key oil handling plant (Source: @UkraineNow/81894).
Russian KAB attacks were launched in northeastern Kharkiv and Donetsk, expanding the strike envelope (Source: @kpszsu/65118).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian use of KAB rockets against civilian infrastructure in central and southern Ukraine demonstrates a willingness to target populated areas, raising the risk of higher civilian casualties. Ukrainian UAV operations have shown increasing effectiveness, successfully neutralising Russian air defence assets and striking critical logistics nodes such as Crimea bridges. The high interception rate reported by Russian air defence indicates intensified aerial contest, but also suggests that Ukrainian drone capabilities remain a significant threat to Russian rear areas and supply lines.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are likely to reinforce air defence around vulnerable cities like Mykolaiv and Vasylkivka while continuing UAV strikes on Russian logistical hubs. Russian forces may increase the deployment of short‑range air defence systems and accelerate KAB launch operations to pressure Ukrainian rear areas. Both sides appear to be escalating aerial engagements, with potential spillover effects on civilian infrastructure and supply routes across the frontlines.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of KAB launch sites in Kharkiv and Donetsk should be prioritized to anticipate further strikes on civilian centers. UAV launch vectors from the Black Sea toward Odesa and Chornomorsk warrant enhanced maritime surveillance. Continued assessment of bridge integrity in Crimea is essential, as repeated Ukrainian attacks could disrupt Russian supply chains and affect operational tempo on the southern front.
